message:
test: read total_exported from support_matrix.json instead of hard-coding it

`claim_reconciliation_detects_readme_drift_and_routes_owner` was using a hard-coded `total_exported=4109` literal as the README marker to mutate. Every time the classified surface grows (which happens on every NIS / NSS / xdr / GLIBC_PRIVATE / fortify-stub porting commit) we have to edit this test in lockstep, or the substitution silently no-ops and the test pretends to pass while testing nothing.

Switch the test to:
1. Read `support_matrix.json` (the single source of truth for total_exported).
2. Parse `total_exported` as a u64.
3. Build the claim string `format!("total_exported={n}")` from that.
4. Use that as the substitution target.

Add a positive assertion (`assert_ne!`) that the post-substitution README content actually differs from the on-disk README; this catches the silent-no-op failure mode where the literal we expected is no longer present in the source.

This stops the test from rotting with every regen and removes one of the two motivations (the previous one was the parse-report helper) that kept dragging this test file into surface-bump commits. After this, only support_matrix.json's `total_exported` field has to move forward — the test follows automatically.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
